notes: Keep the upstream repository beside the Atropos methods paper so trimming claims can resolve to both implementation and publication evidence.
Cutadapt has a stable upstream repository and maintained documentation surface; keep both linked to the canonical adapter-trimming paper.
Archive the NCBI source tree page and link it to the Morgulis et al. SDUST/DustMasker paper root.
Use the upstream GitHub repository as the canonical local evidence packet and pair it with the DIAMOND methods paper.
Prefer the source repository over version-templated release archives and keep the Bioinformatics paper in the linked paper root.

FastQValidator currently resolves through its maintained source repository and release materials rather than a clearly governed canonical methods paper. The VirIdAl pipeline paper cites the fastQValidator repository as tool evidence.
Keep the GitHub source tree and the Babraham project page together; FastQC currently resolves to a software-citation root rather than a formal paper.
The FASTQ surface refers to the seqfu2 upstream repository and its Bioengineering paper.
The current FASTQ surface is seqkit, not seqtk; archive the seqkit repo, docs, and the PLOS One paper together.

SeqKit stats is a FASTQ profiling surface inside the same seqkit upstream project. Keep the nf-core wrapper page as secondary execution evidence, but anchor implementation and publication evidence to the seqkit project itself.
SeqPurge is distributed through ngs-bits; keep the paper beside the repository packet.
UMI-tools has an explicit upstream repository and maintained documentation; keep both paired with the open-access Genome Research paper.
VSEARCH should resolve through the upstream repository and maintained user documentation, paired with the open-access methods paper.
Archive the SourceForge project page or source bundle and keep the publication beside it.
Keep the source repository and the maintained project documentation together with the primary DADA2 methods paper.
Keep AdapterRemoval v2 source evidence paired with the v2 paper because this tool is admitted for trimming, terminal-damage trimming, and paired-end merging.
Archive the Pasteur GitLab source packet and pair it with the 2013 AlienTrimmer methods paper; keep the 2014 Frontiers commentary as supporting sensitivity context.
BayesHammer is distributed inside SPAdes; keep the SPAdes source tree with the canonical Nikolenko et al. BayesHammer paper.
BBDuk is part of BBTools; archive the official tool page and release/download evidence as a software-citation packet.
BBMerge is part of BBTools; keep official tool documentation and the PLOS ONE BBMerge paper together.
Clumpify is part of BBTools; archive the official tool page and BBTools release/download evidence as a software-citation packet.
fastq-scan currently resolves through its source repository and project page rather than a governed canonical methods paper.
FastQ Screen resolves through the maintained repository, Babraham project page, and Wingett and Andrews 2018 paper for multi-genome mapping and quality-control evidence.

FASTX-Toolkit resolves through maintained source/archive evidence and the project homepage for this governed fastx_clipper packet; no standalone fastx_clipper methods paper is currently treated as canonical.
FLASH2 is a maintained successor/fork of FLASH; pair source evidence with the canonical FLASH paper for paired-end merging.
Keep fqtools source evidence paired with the Bioinformatics application note for validation evidence.
Keep leeHom source evidence paired with the Nucleic Acids Research paper for adapter trimming and merge-pairs evidence.
Keep Lighter source evidence paired with the Genome Biology paper for error-correction evidence.
Keep MultiQC source evidence paired with the Bioinformatics paper for QC aggregation evidence.
Archive the Musket release page or source bundle and pair it with the locally archived Bioinformatics paper for error-correction evidence.
Archive the PEAR project page or source bundle and pair it with the Bioinformatics paper for paired-end merging evidence.
PRINSEQ++ is the governed implementation; pair it with the canonical PRINSEQ preprocessing paper and keep the source evidence explicit.
Keep Rcorrector source evidence paired with the GigaScience paper for RNA-seq error-correction evidence.
Seqtk currently resolves through its source repository rather than a governed canonical methods paper; the FastQDesign paper is supporting citation context for repository-based software references.
Keep Skewer source evidence paired with the BMC Bioinformatics paper for adapter trimming evidence.
Trim Galore is a Felix Krueger/Babraham wrapper over Cutadapt/FastQC and currently resolves through source/homepage evidence rather than a separate governed paper.
Archive the Trimmomatic project page or source bundle and pair it with the Bioinformatics paper for adapter and quality trimming evidence.
